column1: # Political Terror Scale (PTS) indicator catalog
# Source: Political Terror Scale (PTS)
#   URL (verified 2026-06-18): https://www.politicalterrorscale.org/Data/Files/PTS-2025.xlsx
# Version: PTS-2025 (data 1976-2024
# License: free academic use; cite Wood
# Attribution: see docs/sources/attributions.md
# This file is the single source of truth for which PTS indicators are
# read in Stage 2. Every Stage 2 PTS adapter call resolves its
# raw_column from this file. Adding/removing an indicator here is the
# only change needed to widen or narrow the PTS ingestion for the prototype.
# Architecture design: docs/architecture/pts.md §3
# --- 3-INDICATOR CHOICE ---
# The PTS xlsx carries 3 parallel score columns (PTS_A from Amnesty
# International
# Department). The catalog extracts all 3 as 3 separate indicators
# (not collapsed to a single worst-of-three score). The justification
# is in the design doc §3: cross-source comparison at Stage 12 needs
# the 3-way granularity (UCDP one-sided and V-Dem repression each
# contribute 2 indicators; PTS contributes 3; the Stage 12 cross-
# source comparison reads all 6 indicators and computes agreement
# per-indicator
# All 3 indicators share these catalog metadata:
#   raw_scale        = "ordinal"        # 1-5 ordinal scale
#   normalized_scale_target = "0-10"    # inverted: 5 (worst terror) -> 0
#   higher_is_better = "0"              # more terror = worse (the 0-10 score is inverted)
#   unit             = "pts_score"
#   rating_category  = "domestic_violence"
# --- SCORE INVERSION (PTS -> 0-10) ---
# The raw PTS scale is 1-5 where 1 = least terror (best) and 5 = most
# terror (worst). The pipeline's scoring convention inverts this:
# higher normalized score = less terror (better). The mapping is:
#   PTS 1 -> 10/10 (least terror)
#   PTS 5 -> 0/10  (most terror)
# The inversion is the Stage 5 score module's responsibility; Stage 2
# only writes the raw 1-5 value to source_observations.normalized_value
# and preserves the "higher_is_better=0" flag so the score module
# inverts the direction. This is the same pattern as the SIPRI-milex
# higher_is_better=False indicators (more spending = worse peace).
# --- MISSING-DATA CONVENTION ---
# The xlsx carries TWO independent signals per cell:
#   PTS_X (str 'NA' or int 1-5) -- the raw score
#   NA_Status_X (int 0/66/77/88/99) -- the provenance flag
# Stage 2 applies the precedence rule: NA_Status takes precedence
# over PTS_X. A cell is "valid data" iff NA_Status_X == 0. The
# 4-case sentinel matrix is in the design doc §6.
# The raw_value audit column preserves the literal cell text:
#   - int 1-5 cells with NA_Status=0: raw_value = str(int)  (e.g.
#   - 'NA' cells with NA_Status != 0: raw_value = "NA"
#   - int cells with NA_Status != 0: raw_value = str(int)  (audit shows the published value; Stage 5 sees the NA_Status and skips)
#   - 'NA' cells with NA_Status=0 (the inconsistency case): raw_value = "NA" + warning logged
# --- REGION CODES ---
# The xlsx uses 7 single-region codes plus 1 multi-region anomaly
# (verified live 2026-06-18; see design doc §6.4):
#   eap (East Asia & Pacific)
#   lac (Latin America & Caribbean)
#   na (North America)
# Plus the data anomaly 'mena
# times for the African Union in 1976-2024; treated as a data entry
# error and passed through verbatim to source_observations.notes).
# These are the World Bank country-and-lending-groups codes; they
# do NOT match the 6-code list in the metadata.json (which was an
# approximation). The architect flags this drift for the developer
# to correct in the same commit as the adapter lands (mirror the
# --- COUNTRY ID COLUMNS ---
# The xlsx carries 4 ID columns per row (COW_Code_A 3-letter alphabetic
# COW_Code_N numeric
# numeric). Stage 2 uses COW_Code_A as the primary source_row_reference
# suffix (matching V-Dem's country_text_id join pattern). Stage 3
# (country match) resolves the COW code to ISO3 via the canonical
# country table.
variable_name
pts_amnesty_score
column8: Political Terror Scale from Amnesty International reports (1-5 scale; higher = more political terror). One of 3 parallel PTS scores per country-year. Cross-validates UCDP one-sided fatalities and V-Dem v2csreprss for the domestic_violence category.

column1: pts_human_rights_watch_score
column8: Political Terror Scale from Human Rights Watch reports (1-5 scale; higher = more political terror). One of 3 parallel PTS scores per country-year. Less coverage than PTS_A (HRW has fewer country-years coded; NA_Status=88 'not coded' is common).

column1: pts_state_dept_score
column8: Political Terror Scale from US State Department human rights reports (1-5 scale; higher = more political terror). One of 3 parallel PTS scores per country-year. Highest coverage of the 3 (State reports cover nearly all countries every year).
